meta: Mole tunnels sit 1 to 20 inches deep. Feeding tunnels run 1-4 inches under the surface; permanent deep runs go 6-20+ inches, following foundations and edges.

title: Why Do Moles Make Molehills? | Got Moles Blog | Got Moles
meta: Molehills are dirt-disposal mounds. As a mole tunnels, the displaced soil gets pushed up a vertical shaft. Each mound marks a tunnel — not a mole.

meta: Moles target yards with moist, loose soil and abundant earthworms. Irrigated PNW lawns tick every box — which is why mole activity here is relentless.

title: How Many Babies Do Moles Have? | Got Moles Blog | Got Moles
meta: Townsend's moles have 2 to 4 pups per litter, once a year, born March-April. Pups leave the nest around 6 weeks and disperse to claim territory.

title: Do Moles Live in Groups? | Got Moles Blog | Got Moles
meta: No — moles are solitary. Each adult Townsend's mole defends its own tunnel network and only tolerates another mole during the brief winter mating window.

title: Are Moles Poisonous or Venomous? | Got Moles Blog | Got Moles
meta: Washington mole species are not venomous. Some mole species worldwide produce mild toxins for paralyzing worms, but Townsend's and Pacific Coast moles don't.
